title: Reply with exactly: reviewer OK
summary_text: **Findings (stale detection safety)** 1. **High:** stale release ignores tmux/compact liveness for active claims. Current release gate is only `!live_pid && age>=ttl` at [kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh:337). If `run_driver_pid` is missing but tmux is alive and compact is fresh, it can still release and archive. 2. **High:** false stale release is destructive because it immediately archives compact/workspace artifacts after release at [kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh:340). Safer: require `tmux_dead && compact_stale` before release+archive. 3. **Medium:** stale age relies on `updated_at_epoch` only ([kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh:269), [kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh:337)). Corrupt/zero epoch makes claims instantly stale even if compact was recently updated. 4. **Medium:** orphan compact archival correctly checks compact freshness ([kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh:357)), but active-claim stale path does not use that same compact freshness guard, creating inconsistent safety behavior. 5. **Test gap:** existing test forces stale by zeroing `updated_at_epoch` ([test-kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh:52)) but has no coverage for the safest edge cases: - `pid missing + tmux alive + compact fresh` => **must not release/archive** - `pid missing + tmux dead + compact fresh` => **defer/retry, no archive** - `pid missing + tmux dead + compact stale` => **release + archive**
